compiled_state.title: Create a KNOWN ISSUES for this project

comments.body:
## Context
Claude Code agents working on the DSO plugin repeatedly encounter the same operational friction — tool timeout ceilings that silently kill commands, path confusion in worktrees, review gate deadlocks for sub-agents — and spend cycles rediscovering workarounds that prior sessions already found. CLAUDE.md references a `.claude/docs/KNOWN-ISSUES.md` file for debugging, but the file doesn't exist for this project. The onboarding skill also tries to copy a template from a path that doesn't exist (`plugins/dso/docs/templates/KNOWN-ISSUES.md`), so host projects never get one either.

## Success Criteria
1. `.claude/docs/KNOWN-ISSUES.md` exists with incident entries covering these 10 topics: tool timeout ceiling (exit 144), path confusion in worktrees, broad test commands killed by timeout, worktree venv/command-not-found, review gate blocks sub-agent commits, sub-agent nesting causes tool-result errors, hook failure cascades, ticket index merge conflicts, `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` unbound in parallel execution, cascading failure runaway
2. Each issue follows the incident format (ID, keywords, symptom, root cause, detection, fix) so agents can search by keyword and find actionable guidance
3. The onboarding template pipeline works end-to-end: `plugins/dso/docs/templates/KNOWN-ISSUES.md` exists as a generic template, and onboarding copies it as a verbatim copy to host projects at `.claude/docs/KNOWN-ISSUES.md`
4. The Common Fixes table in CLAUDE.md is replaced with a single-line pointer: `See .claude/docs/KNOWN-ISSUES.md for common operational fixes and workarounds.` — scoped to that table only, no other CLAUDE.md restructuring
5. The old `templates/KNOWN-ISSUES.example.md` is removed to avoid path confusion
6. A grep-based smoke test confirms that searching KNOWN-ISSUES.md for each of the 10 incident keywords (e.g., `exit 144`, `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT`, `cascading failure`) returns a matching incident entry with a fix section

## Scenario Analysis
- **Ordering/atomicity of file changes** (`high`, `deployment`): If CLAUDE.md pointer, template, or KNOWN-ISSUES.md land in separate commits, agents in the window between commits lose access to operational guidance. All changes must land atomically.
- **Host project KNOWN-ISSUES overwritten on re-onboarding** (`high`, `deployment`): If onboarding runs again on a host project that already has a populated KNOWN-ISSUES.md, the copy step overwrites project-specific history with the generic template.
- **Worktree path resolution for CLAUDE.md pointer** (`medium`, `runtime`): The pointer references `.claude/docs/KNOWN-ISSUES.md` which resolves correctly in worktrees (`.claude/` is at worktree root via git), but agents may still need explicit guidance to use repo-root-relative paths.

## Dependencies
None

## Approach
Create `.claude/docs/KNOWN-ISSUES.md` with 10 incident entries covering the triaged issues. Create the generic template at `plugins/dso/docs/templates/KNOWN-ISSUES.md`. Replace the CLAUDE.md Common Fixes table with a single-line reference. Remove `templates/KNOWN-ISSUES.example.md`. All changes land atomically in a single commit.

### Planning Intelligence Log

- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 3 (capped from 6 in reduced mode)
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: none
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Adversarial review: 5 findings, 4 accepted. Full exchange: /tmp/workflow-plugin-1a40ba26f6a56925/adversarial-review-known-issues-dso-plugin.json
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Epic complete: all 4 stories closed, all 6 success criteria verified by completion-verifier
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
